Football.title: National League club release angry statement as player features in Baller League without permission
Football.content:
National League club release angry statement as player features in Baller League without permission

National League side Wealdstone have released a statement following one of their players' involvement in Baller League.

Sak Hassan, who is contracted to Wealdstone, spent Monday night competing in the new season of Baller League.

2 Wealdstone are fuming that their player featured for KSI's team in Baller League Credit: Getty

Hassan turned out for KSI's new team, Prime FC, where he scored the opener against N5 FC.

But Wealdstone aren't happy and revealed they didn't give their player permission to play in the six-a-side competition.

In a statement, they said: "We are aware that Sak Hassan, under contract with Wealdstone FC, appeared in this evening’s Baller League.

"The club was not approached for permission for Sak to participate, nor were we made aware of his involvement in advance.

"We will now be following up with both the Baller League and the player directly.

"No further comment will be made until those discussions have taken place."

It's clear the player took part without letting the National League club know.

And in an interview during the broadcast, Chunkz asked KSI how he got Hassan to play for his team on Monday night.

KSI replied: "You mentioned him to me, so I talked to him and I was like, 'bro, I got a big thing coming.'

"At this point, I didn't mention Daggers...I was like bro, I've got big things...so, there is that, and then also said, bro, think of the clout."

Fans unhappy

It's unclear what will happen to Hassan now, but in the comments, plenty of Wealdstone fans were furious.

One said: "Clearly not a team player with no respect for our club. Mickey Mouse Football over National League Football."

Another added: "He's just thrown a huge opportunity away with only himself to blame there."

"The stones are bigger than one player that barely ever plays, if he cares more about @BallerLeagueUK then he can make a permanent move to them! Stones have bigger and better things to worry about!" a third wrote.

Meanwhile, a fourth asked: "Why does he needs to ask for permission to play in a 6-a-side tournament?"

And a fifth fan said: "He’s just having a kickabout with some amateur level players on YouTube hardly a big deal."

2 KSI's team got off to a great start with Hassan playing Credit: Baller League

KSI's new venture

Not only is KSI a manager in Baller League, he is also now a co-owner at Wealdstone's former National League rivals Dagenham and Redbridge.

KSI is officially a shareholder and strategic partner at the club, and aims to take them to the Premier League.

But first, promotion from the National League South needs to be achieved.

The Daggers are more than likely looking at next season for that because they are 12th at the moment, nine points off the final play-off spot.

If they were to get promoted to the National League, they would likely come up against Wealdstone next season.

And that could be an awkward moment for KSI and Hassan after their secret dealings at Baller League's opening night.

Football.title: Wayne Rooney makes Max Dowman prediction and urges Arsenal to ‘keep him away from public’
Football.content:
Wayne Rooney makes Max Dowman prediction and urges Arsenal to ‘keep him away from public’

Wayne Rooney believes education may take precedence over football for Max Dowman despite making Premier League history for Arsenal.

The teenage starlet became the youngest ever goalscorer in the top flight on Saturday when he netted in their 2-0 win over Everton.

3 Dowman announced himself to the Premier League with his goal at the weekend Credit: Getty

At the age of 16 years and 73 days, Dowman broke the long-standing record that was claimed by James Vaughan at Everton in 2005.

He has now made seven senior appearances for Arsenal, one of which came in the Champions League league phase earlier in the season.

Dowman has even been tipped for a wildcard selection in England's World Cup squad this summer.

However, Rooney, who famously burst onto the scene at 16-years-old for Everton, has hinted that his studies could get in the way.

Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League table with seven matches to play, and the title race could go to the wire.

"He has got his GCSEs, and his education, I'm sure for him and his family, is important as well," Rooney said on his BBC show.

"What an opportunity for him to be around the squad. He could be a Premier League winner when he's still finishing school off.

"He's got an aura and a belief about him and it was the same against Everton.

"If it's education first, I don't think we'll see much of him over the next few weeks."

Rooney added: "He won't be in as much as he'd probably like, but then you've got many years ahead of you."

3 Rooney believes school could limit Dowman's game time in the upcoming weeks Credit: Getty

Away from the cameras

Host Kelly Somers also mentioned how she hasn't seen Dowman conduct any interviews during his impressive rise at Arsenal.

She asked Rooney if they are taking the right approach in this aspect, and the Premier League legend have them a key piece of advice.

"He'll probably do some internal ones with Arsenal, just to get him used to it," the Manchester United icon said.

He added: "I think nowadays, the younger lads have got social media, they're on it all the time anyway.

"So, I'm sure he'll probably be okay doing interviews. But, I think it's best just to keep him away from the public for this period."

What next for Dowman?

Dowman could have had plenty more minutes to his name this season if it weren't for an ankle injury that sidelined him for weeks.

3 As things stand, Dowman could be set for a stunning career in football Credit: Getty

But head coach Mikel Arteta could call upon his help on Tuesday night when they face Bayer Leverkusen in the Champions League.

The aggregate score in their last 16 tie is currently 1-1, and the second leg will come live on talkSPORT as the Gunners eye the quarter-finals.

Dowman could then win his first piece of silverware in Arsenal colours on Sunday afternoon in the Carabao Cup final.

They take on Premier League title rivals Manchester City at Wembley Stadium, and this match will also be live on talkSPORT.

Football.title: New footage shows fan in tears after ‘moment of madness’ as EFL star kicks bottle at their head
Football.content:
Jordan Thompson could face punishment after kicking a water bottle at the head of a Norwich City supporter.

The midfielder was substituted in the 65th minute for Preston North End during their 2-0 Championship defeat on Saturday.

3 Thompson booted a bottle in anger and it hit a fan on the head Credit: @SkyFootball X

Footage posted by Sky Sports shows Thompson walking off the pitch at Carrow Road and shaking hands with club staff.

Before sitting on the bench, the Northern Ireland international sees a bottle on the ground and kicks it in apparent frustration.

It flies into the stand and hits the head of an unsuspecting Norwich fan who was having a drink at the time.

Some visibly furious supporters turned towards Thompson, with one man in particular appearing to mouth expletives in his direction.

Thompson is then seen standing closer to the fans and appears to apologise to the affected supporter, who seemed to be in tears.

No action was taken at the time, but the Football Association could charge the 29-year-old for his actions.

Former Premier League referee Dermot Gallagher reviewed the bottle kick on Ref Watch for Sky Sports, and said: "Why would you do this?

"Whether he'll be reprimanded today, I don't know, but you look at that... the referee hasn't seen it because the substitution has been made, the assistant has got his back to it.

"So, he's lucky he doesn't get seen because it would be a red card on the day, without doubt. But doing that is just so dangerous, you see it just hit somebody.

"Somebody was having a drink as well, wasn't aware. It's a moment of madness."

3 Thompson waved his hand to apologise Credit: @SkyFootball X

3 Many Norwich fans were left angered with the Preston man Credit: @SkyFootball X

Booting the bottle

Thompson certainly isn't the first man on the touchline to take their anger out on a water bottle mid-match.

In 2018, Unai Emery was fined £8,000 by the FA after kicking a bottle at a Brighton fan during his managerial tenure at Arsenal.

Emery, who was charged with improper conduct, apologised and shook their hand at full-time.

Pep Guardiola was also involved in a similar incident whilst in the dugout for Manchester City against Leeds United in 2022.

He accidentally kicked it towards the Leeds bench and appeared to hit one of their members of staff.

An embarrassed Guardiola then immediately ran over to apologise.

Football.title: Referee caught in viral Chelsea huddle would ‘rather not be in the news’ after awkward moment
Football.content:
Referee caught in viral Chelsea huddle would ‘rather not be in the news’ after awkward moment

Former Premier League referee Graham Scott has defended Paul Tierney for his involvement in the Chelsea huddle at the weekend.

Before they lost to Newcastle at home for the first time since 2012 on Saturday, the Blues had their pre-match huddle on the halfway line.

4 Tierney was baffled with what was going on at Stamford Bridge Credit: Getty

Referee Tierney ended up being caught in the middle, leading to a bizarre image of him and the players going viral.



It wasn't a situation that could be taken too seriously, with Cole Palmer putting his arm on the referee and laughing throughout.

And when asked on Hawksbee and Jacobs if he would have allowed that to happen, Scott said: "I'd like to think I would have found a way out, as Andy was just saying, it is awkward and embarrassing.

"Chelsea could have made his life a lot easier by just creating a space and stepping aside.

"But I'd like to think I would have found a way out. I have sympathy for Paul because he gets caught up in the middle of it, and then, it's too late.



"Then you don't know how long it is going to go on, and you've got a camera on you.

"I would have made a bit of a scene and a fuss to make it clear I wasn't too impressed by the whole thing.



"I am waiting for Chelsea's opponents to do the same thing at the same thing - that would be really interesting."

Then, when asked whether referees like being at the centre of these things or not, he replied: "No, definitely not.



"Paul, in particular, doesn't - he'd much rather be in the background.

4 Gordon scored as Newcastle saw off Chelsea 1-0 in the game on Saturday Credit: Getty

"There are a few refs who do seem to like to be the centre of attention more than they should, but there are a few who keep a lower profile.

"He is certainly one who would rather not be in the news for this kind of thing."

Alternative view

In contrast to what Scott said about Tierney, Darren Bent believes the referee could have made an effort to get out of the huddle, and that he did actually enjoy the attention.



Speaking on Drive, he said: “I don’t mind them doing the huddle around the ball, but the referee does not need to be in there, that’s the worst part about the whole thing.

“Paul Tierney, just get out of there! Just get out, what are you doing in there?

“Surely he would go: ‘Lads, come on, stop being stupid, let me out of here.’ He wanted to stay there with that smirk on his face…he loved it.”

4 Scott believes Tierney will hate the attention while Bent thinks he loved it Credit: Getty

'Blown out of proportion'

After the incident, Liam Rosenior explained why his team have started to strangely huddle around the ball before kick-off.



The Chelsea manager said: “I'm respectful to the game.

“My players made the decision that they wanted to be around the ball, to respect the ball and show unity and leadership.

"That is not my decision. That was a decision between the leadership group and the team.

“There is nothing they're doing with that huddle that is disrespectful to the opposition. We had a meeting with the referee. My assistant goes in. The first thing he (Tierney) talks about is our huddle. He says about when I complained about Arsenal being in our half.

“We're not being disrespectful to the opposition. If Paul had focused more on his job, which was to make the right decisions, we have a penalty. I don't think anybody in this room can say that Woltemade doesn't kick Cole Palmer down in the box.

4 Rosenior has come under criticism for his comments regarding Tierney and the huddle Credit: Getty



“So let's focus on the things that are important. My team showing unity is not as important as getting the decisions right on the pitch.”

And ahead of the Blues taking on PSG in the Champions League, he has revealed that his side are considering a change in where they hold their pre-match huddles.

He said: “I think in terms of the huddle, I think it's been blown out of proportion, to be honest.

“It's a small, small thing amongst many, many serious things that we need to address, such as a massive game against PSG tomorrow.

“In terms of the huddle, the lads have always wanted to show unity and togetherness. I think they'll continue to do that.



“What we don't want to do is antagonise or bring the noise on ourselves. And we'll make the decision on where we do that on the pitch tomorrow.”

Football.title: Chelsea warned they are set to face further sanctions after being hit with transfer ban
Football.content:
Chelsea warned they are set to face further sanctions after being hit with transfer ban

Chelsea are set to face more sanctions from the footballing authorities as part of an investigation from the Football Association.

The Blues have been punished for breaching Premier League rules on Financial Reporting, Third Party Investment and Youth Development.

4 Chelsea have been hit with a fine and suspended transfer ban by the Premier League Credit: Getty

Chelsea have accepted a £10.75million fine, and been hit with a one-year first team player transfer ban suspended for two years.

An immediate nine-month academy transfer ban has also been given.

Self-reporting of the breaches by Todd Boehly and Clearlake Capital came into consideration when deciding the punishment's severity.

However, the FA have conducted a separate disciplinary process that relates to alleged breaches of similar conduct.

A total of 74 charges have been brought against the Premier League club, who could therefore be dealt with further punishment.

"It's broadly payments to agents, payments to other parties and, sort of, incentives to people to persuade players to come to Chelsea," football finance expert Kieran Maguire told talkSPORT.

"This was very much during the (Roman) Abramovich era. I think the type of players that were involved, who have sort of been cited... and there's been nothing done wrong by the players, by the way.

"They will have simply just signed a form, they will have wanted to join Chelsea. But I think Eden Hazard has been mentioned, Willian has been mentioned, Samuel Eto'o.

"It's trying to persuade, perhaps, other people to encourage their clients or their representatives to allow the players to come to the club.

"And those payments have been made through opaque contracts and opaque accounts.

4 Chelsea could be set for more sanctions due to a separate FA process Credit: Getty

"I think it's also a function of the fact that Roman Abramovich, whilst he invested huge amounts into Chelsea, he was doing this via companies based in the British Virgin Islands and Cayman Islands, and so on.

"And it's often difficult to follow the crumb trail of cash that's been paid. Again, looking at a sort of quick review of the Premier League report, there's been no accusation of fraud.

"It's more to do with that there's an awful lot of pieces of paperwork which didn't necessarily join up particularly well together."

News of these charges broke in September, with their investigation expected to be concluded imminently.

However, despite the potential for further serious punishment, talkSPORT understands that the FA are set to follow the Premier League with a large fine for the club.

They will receive no sporting sanction for their 74 charges.

4 Clearlake and Boehly self-reported the breaches for Chelsea Credit: Getty

Self-reporting aided Chelsea

talkSPORT reported in March 2025 that Chelsea were negotiating a financial settlement with the Premier League over the breaches.

They argued that they shouldn't be slapped with a points deduction due to Abramovich no longer being the owner.

Chelsea self-declared and cooperated with the league, just like they did with UEFA before they paid £8m over a similar issue in 2023.

Their self-reporting of the matter appears to have paid dividends as they avoided a points deduction.

When asked if Chelsea will be quietly pleased that their punishment from the Premier League wasn't worse, Maguire said: "Yes.

"I think, first of all, Chelsea will be delighted that it's a fine rather than a points deduction. I think it shows the merits of Chelsea self-reporting themselves.

4 Chelsea were able to avoid a points deduction for the Premier League breaches Credit: Getty

"When Clearlake Capital acquired the club (in 2022), this was one of the things that they discovered during a period of due diligence.

"And therefore, on the back of that, Chelsea have been confident all along that it would be a financial penalty.

"Clearlake, they held back around about £150m from the buying price for Chelsea so therefore a £10m fine, I think they will be pleased with.

"And I'm sure they're also very confident that the transfer ban, because it's suspended, the new owners are confident that they've done nothing wrong, so they feel it will be unlikely that that will be imposed."

Football.title: Mikel Arteta issues update as Arsenal suffer major injury blow just a week before Carabao Cup final
Football.content:
Mikel Arteta has issued a worrying update after Jurrien Timber dealt Arsenal a major scare at the weekend.

The Gunners' right back was forced off with injury in the first half of their 2-0 win against Everton at the Emirates.

3 Timber went off in the first half after failing to overcome injury Credit: Getty

He started his 28th Premier League match of the season on Saturday as the Gunners welcomed the Toffees to north London.



But he failed to last the opening 45 minutes, being withdrawn in the 37th minute due to a possible groin injury.

Timber, who has now featured in 43 games across all competitions this term, went down in the early stages.

The Dutchman required medical attention, and while he continued playing, he couldn't carry on.

Jur joking

Mikel Arteta decided to bring on natural centre-back Christhian Mosquera for the former Ajax star.



And it could have been worse for Arsenal after William Saliba needed medical treatment towards the end of the first half.

The French centre-back was clattered into by David Raya as the Spaniard punched away an Everton delivery.



Despite looking dazed on the deck, he managed to continue and came out for the second half, which saw Viktor Gyokeres and Max Dowman score late on.

On Monday, Arteta then addressed the issue with Timber and gave a concerning update about his fitness ahead of the Carabao Cup final, which is quickly coming up on Sunday afternoon.



When asked whether his defender and captain Martin Odegaard would be missing for the Gunners' Champions League last 16 second leg on Tuesday evening, he said: "Yes, both of them are out."

3 Timber has been an ever-present figure for the Gunners so far this campaign Credit: Getty

The confirmation of their absence is not a good sign for their chance of playing at Wembley against Manchester City this weekend.

But despite losing some key stars, Arteta hailed the impact of his substitutes against Everton when speaking post-match, indicating he'd be happy with his replacements if they are needed.

He said: "All of them - Gabi [Martinelli], Mosquera had to come in when Jurrien was out and I think it's been the theme of the season.



"I think the impact of the finishers that the players have played is the reason why we are here, basically.

"That says a lot about the people that we have in the dressing room.

"Because sometimes my decision, maybe they're not right or they're not fair, they take it in the right way with only one objective, which is to help the team to win."

3 Arteta will be sweating over the severity of Timber's knock ahead of this week's Carabao Cup final Credit: AFP

Terrible timing

The worrying blow to Timber came just eight days out from Arsenal facing Man City in next Sunday's Carabao Cup final.

They are looking to win the competition for the first time since 1993.



And the club also have their huge Champions League clash against Bayer Leverkusen on the horizon next.

The Gunners were held to a 1-1 draw in the first leg of their last-16 tie in Germany midweek.

Should they emerge victorious in the second leg, they will face the winner of Bodo/Glimt vs Sporting Lisbon.

As it stands, it's looking as though Bodo will be in the quarter-finals after easing to a 3-0 win in Norway.

News.title: Banksy’s ‘real name finally revealed in old police report’ after famed graffiti artist’s ‘misdemeanour’ in New York
News.content:
BANKSY UNMASKED Banksy’s ‘real name finally revealed in old police report’ after famed graffiti artist’s ‘misdemeanour’ in New York

BANKSY’S identity has reportedly finally been uncovered in a 26-year-old police report.

The real name of the world’s most famous graffiti artist is said to be Robin Gunningham after records of a misdemeanour in New York in 2000 were found.





It has long been speculated that this was Banksy’s real name but news agency Reuters believe they can finally confirm his identity after an investigation.

It also claimed that Gunningham changed his legal name to David Jones – one of the UK’s most common – to avoid detection.



Under this new name he travelled to Ukraine where Banksy painted murals. He left in October 2022 on the same day as Robert del Naja a member of Massive Attack thought to be in the artist’s circle.

It was even once suggested that Robert was the artist himself as he is also a graffiti artist.

Reuters also found that the date on Jones’s passport matched the one on Gunningham’s documents.

Banksy emerged out of the Bristol art scene in the late 1990s and his identity has remained a mystery throughout his thirty year career.



It was previously reported that Robin Gunningham, a student at Bristol Cathedral School, adopted the name Robin Banks before becoming Banksy.

Even those who have parted ways with the famous graffiti artist, such as his former manager Steve Lazarides, have kept his name a secret.

Reuters said there was a public interest in knowing the identity of any public figure who has an influence on discourse.

The street artist’s stencil paintings are known for their sharp social commentary and have become incredibly valuable.

Banksy has said nothing following the investigation but his lawyer Mark Stephens said he didn’t accept “many of the details contained within your inquiry are correct”.



The representative added that the report could put the artist in danger and would interfere with his work.

Banksy was first named as Robin Gunningham in 2008 in a newspaper article but his then-manager denied it.

Gunningham was arrested in September 2000 after a Manhattan billboard for Marc Jacobs was defaced and he confessed, Reuters reports.

The “artist” had given the model “goofy teeth” and drawn a “giant speech bubble” but he didn’t get the chance to fill it before police arrested him.

Police documents, a handwritten confession and court filings from the time identify Gunningham as the vandal, the report found.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

The confession reads: ““The evening the night of September 17th I had been out drinking at a nightclub with friends when I decide to make a humorous adjustment to a billboard on top of the property on Hudson st.”

Gunningham posted bail for $1,500 and the felony offences were reduced to a misdemeanour charge. After a $310 fine and five days of community service he was good to go.

News.title: Fury as hundreds of mafia prisoners including murderers and terrorists to be dumped on holiday island loved by Brits
News.content:
CRIMINAL COLONY Fury as hundreds of mafia prisoners including murderers and terrorists to be dumped on holiday island loved by Brits

MOBSTERS and convicted terrorists will soon be dumped on one of Italy’s paradise islands on the Mediterranean Sea.

Frequently visited by Brits, Sardinia is famous for its white sandy beaches and turquoise bays – a postcard-perfect image that could be tarnished by hundreds of maximum-security prisoners.





Under the government of Giorgia Meloni, the island has been ordered to take in about 250 top-level mafia bosses, killers, and domestic terrorists.

This represents a third of the 750 high-risk inmates being consolidated into the handful of maximum-security facilities.

The plan will see them arrive in the next few weeks – despite protests by locals, who do not want the island to revert back to being seen as a hub for the “highest echelons” of Italy‘s mobsters.

“Sardinia does not deserve to be seen as Italy‘s Cayenne,” said governor Alessandra Todde, invoking the notorious former French Guiana penal colony on Devil’s Island.

“We have always done our bit in the fight against the mafia and organised crime, but we cannot accept such an unfair distribution [of prisoners].

“These are men who represent the highest echelons of mafia organisations, from the ’Ndrangheta to the Camorra, and they will have a profound impact on the island.”

In February, a rally saw more than 1,500 people – regional administrators, mayors, trade unions, associations and citizens – gather in Sardinia against the plan.

Todde told La Repubblica that she had been appealing for months to stop the transfer, but to no avail.



“We have written to Giorgia Meloni, but we’ve had no response,” she said. We are not asking for special privileges – just respect.”

The inmates will be split up between three jails – in the capital, Cagliari, the central city of Nuoro and Sassari in the north – and kept in strict near-total isolation under Italy’s controversial 41bis regime.

Named after an article of the Italian criminal code, it is among the most restrictive in Europe.

Introduced in their current form in 1992, after the mafia killings of judges Giovanni Falcone and Paolo Borsellino, the measures were originally meant to cut off mafia bosses from the outside world and other inmates, and to stop them from ordering criminal acts from behind bars.

While the restrictions are usually reserved for mobsters, they can now be imparted on people who have been found guilty of crimes ranging from terrorism to tobacco smuggling and kidnapping.



There is also the fear that the government’s plan could push mafia clans to relocate from mainland Italy to be near their jailed relatives.

This would be creating opportunities to launder illicit money and infiltrate business, particularly in less developed areas.

Maria Cristina Ornano, head of the sentence enforcement tribunal in Cagliari, said police and the judiciary would need increased security resources if more mobsters arrive.

“Once organised crime takes root here, we will not be able to get rid of it,” she said last month.

“We can see it in parts of southern Italy, which are among the most economically and socially deprived areas.”



Tourism accounts for just 7 per cent of output in the Nuoro region, 2025 Chamber of Commerce data show, where the economy mainly relies on agriculture and is dominated by small firms.

Pietro Borrotzu, a Catholic priest who runs a rehabilitation cooperative for prisoners in Nuoro, said dangerous working conditions and low salaries provide an ideal environment for the clans.

He said: “In this kind of context, organised crime could find plenty of foot soldiers.”

Business lobby Confindustria described what will be happening to Nuoro as “punishment.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Pierpaolo Milia, the group’s local head, said: “We are more of an island than Sardinia itself, far from ports and airports.



“Business incentives would be needed, and instead we are being punished with 41bis inmates.”

News.title: Is Britain hooked on government handouts we can’t afford? VOTE NOW
News.content:
POLL OF THE DAY Is Britain hooked on government handouts we can’t afford? VOTE NOW

SIR Keir Starmer has announced an emergency £53m support package for “vulnerable” households hit by the spike in the price of heating oil.

The PM said yesterday that the funds will go to “those households that are most exposed” to eye-watering price hikes.



As turmoil in the Middle East drives up global oil prices, Brits face increasing pressure on household budgets.

Funding has been allocated based on census data, “reflecting where the greatest need is.”



If the war on Iran continues to rage on, the PM could face even louder calls for a package of Government cash for households struggling with their gas and electricity bills.

With Britain already drowning in more than £2.8trillion of debt, questions remain about whether the UK can afford further handouts.

So what do YOU think, is Britain hooked on government handouts we can’t afford? Have your say in our exclusive poll.

Love to debate? Join Sun Club for just £1.99 a month for the boldest opinion on the issues shaping Britain.

News.title: Shocking moment drunk woman falls out of moving taxi onto busy road & ends up under wheel after fighting with friend
News.content:
THIS is the shocking moment a drunk woman falls out of a moving cab onto a busy road before getting run over by the taxi.

Horrifying footage shows the rear passenger door being pushed open as the taxi drives along a four-lane road.





A pair of kicking legs emerge and seconds later the driver turns on the car’s hazard lights while steering the vehicle towards the side of the road.

The woman appears to struggle before she tumbles out of the car and lands on the tarmac.

Her arm disappears for a moment under one of the taxi’s wheels as she lies in the middle of the busy road.



Dashcam footage then shows the car come to a stop as someone steps out.

Police said the incident happened in Xi’an, in central China on March 12.

Cops received a report after the footage went viral.

They said the woman, known by the surname Cheng, had been travelling in the taxi with a female friend after the pair had been drinking.



The two women began arguing during the journey, police said.

The woman who fell suffered minor abrasions, according to a police statement issued on 13th March.

Officials said the pair later continued their journey in the same taxi after the incident.

Police added that the case had been ruled out as a criminal matter.



But Cheng was slapped with a penalty for jumping from a moving vehicle by traffic cops.

It comes after footage of a toddler tumbling out of a moving car on a busy road in California went viral earlier this year.

Terrifying dashcam video showed the 19-month-old baby flying out of a moving car.

The passenger door flapped open as the car came to a sudden stop, almost causing a crash with a car behind it.

The baby was then seen sitting on the road in the middle of a busy junction, defenceless.



A car stopped just inches away from the small child before the tot’s mum leapt out of her vehicle and grabbed the baby.

News.title: Iran security chief & top war commander are KILLED after Israeli assassination strikes in biggest scalps since Ayatollah
News.content:
ISRAEL said it killed the commander running Iran’s war machine and the mastermind of bloody protest crackdowns today.

Security chief Ali Larijani, 67, is widely believed to have assumed command of military operations following the decapitation of the mullah regime.





But Israel’s Defence Minister Israel Katz said he had joined other Iranian leaders “in the depths of hell” today.

It comes as…

The Israel Defence Forces (IDF) issued a statement confirming Larijani’s death following overnight reports that he had been targeted.

The powerful secretary of Iran’s National Security Council is believed to have taken charge following the assassination of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and the wounding of his son Mojtaba.

In another major coup for Israeli and US operations, the IDF said a separate strike killed Basij paramilitary force commander Gholamreza Soleimani, 62.

Katz said: “I have just been updated by the Chief of Staff that Larijani, Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, and the head of the Basij – Iran‘s central repression apparatus – [Soleimani], were eliminated last night.

“Larijani and the Basij commander… joined the head of the annihilation program, Khamenei, and all the eliminated members of the axis of evil, in the depths of hell.”

Larijani, a former nuclear negotiator and a close ally of Khamenei, was seen in Tehran on Friday taking part in Quds Day rallies while taunting Donald Trump.



He told local media as Israeli missiles rained down behind the demonstrations: “These attacks are out of fear, out of desperation.

“One who is strong wouldn’t bomb demonstrations at all. It’s clear that it has failed.”

A close crony of the late Ayatollah Khamenei, Larijani is widely believed to have assumed command of military operations following the decapitation of the regime.



He was called the most powerful man in Iran and the country’s de facto leader following the assassination of Khamenei on February 28.

On Monday, he directly addressed Trump on social media, penning: “While starting a war is easy, it cannot be won with a few tweets.

“We will not relent until making you sorry for this grave miscalculation.”

Tehran has appeared to deny claims that the top security chief is dead.

The regime posted a handwritten note it claimed was written by Larijani on his social media account minutes after Israel’s claim.



It said: “On the occasion of the funeral ceremony for the valiant martyrs of the Islamic Republic of Iran’s Navy: Their memory will forever remain in the heart of the Iranian nation, and these martyrdoms will strengthen the foundation of the Islamic Republic’s Army for years to come within the structure of the armed forces.”

Soleimani’s Basij group is known for violently suppressing anti-regime protests.

The tyrannical commander ordered the brutal January purge feared to have killed at least 30,000 people rising up against the Islamist state.



The IDF said on X shortly before Larijani was pronounced dead: “In a precise strike in Tehran: IDF eliminates the commander of the Basij unit.

“Guided by precise intelligence from Military Intelligence, the Air Force conducted a targeted strike yesterday in the heart of Tehran, eliminating Gholam Reza Soleimani, commander of the Basij unit over the past six years.”

Soleimani died alongside top lieutenants in a targeted overnight missile strike on their tented HQ.



In other strikes, top Palestinian Islamic Jihad leader Akram al-Ajouri and other top officials in the terror group were targeted by the IDF.

Israel believes al-Ajouri was killed in the strike.

The reeling Iranian regime lashed out at countries across the Middle East over Monday night – with strikes reported in Iraq, the UAE and Qatar among others.

US air defence systems gunned down drones in Baghdad – with shocking footage showing American C-RAM blasters in action.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

President Trump on Monday threatened Nato with “a very bad future” if they did not agree to help unblock the Strait of Hormuz.



Iran have held their chokehold on the key waterway over the last few weeks – plunging global oil prices into crisis.

More to follow… For the latest news on this story keep checking back at The Sun Online
